programme.title: Quo Vadis TV
programme.desc: Quo Vadis TV is committed to sharing the Biblical message in a way that makes Christian living practical, realistic, and attainable. Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

programme.title: Morning Call

programme.title: Squawk on the Street
programme.desc: Taking viewers onto the floor of the exchange, right to the posts where the big companies in the world trade; tick charts track the action of every trade of every stock; the treasury, currency and commodity action in real time.

programme.title: The Exchange
programme.desc: Anchor Kelly Evans reports on the important stories to markets and investors; reporters provide actionable information to viewers as they make daily investment decisions.

programme.title: Bonanza
programme.desc: Ben Cartwright is the patriarch of an all-male Nevada ranching family. Set during and after the Civil War, "Bonanza" is the story of life on the family's thousand-acre spread, known as the Ponderosa, near Virginia City. Ben has his three sons, each with a different (and deceased) mother. Serious eldest son Adam hopes to follow in his father's footsteps; middle son Hoss is a gentle giant -- physically strong, but sometimes naive; and youngest son Little Joe is the most impetuous and romantic of the offspring.

programme.title: Mathis Court With Judge Mathis
programme.desc: Former friends end up in court after an unpaid loan leads to social media drama and slashed tires; a man sues after he's attacked by a dog while biking.

programme.title: Mathis Court With Judge Mathis
programme.desc: A man blames his neighbor for tricking him into paying for the removal of a ghost that didn't exist; an actor sues after being fired over a costume disagreement.

programme.title: Justice for the People With Judge Milian
programme.desc: A pair of fathers and sons battle it out in court over fake shoes; a neighborly dispute results in a damaged windshield.

programme.title: Justice for the People With Judge Milian
programme.desc: A woman blames her pesky neighbor for ruining a baby shower for her godchild; a dispute between co-workers over a stolen lunch.

programme.title: Supreme Justice With Judge Karen
programme.desc: A naturalist sues a photographer for refusing to take photos of his nudist community; a cake artist seeks payment for a decorating party.

programme.title: Supreme Justice With Judge Karen
programme.desc: Dispute between friends over a wig that is lost at an amusement park; Pilates instructor sues to recoup fees after a former client quits without notice.

programme.title: Justice for All With Judge Cristina Pérez
programme.desc: A woman sues because a bar mitzvah guest insisted on invitations for her family, then none of them attended; a dispute between cousins over unpaid rent.

programme.title: Hope and Health With Mathew Embry
programme.desc: Angie Gensler is a mother and personal trainer who was diagnosed with multiple sclerosis in 2004. In this episode, Mathew Embry speaks with Angie about her diagnosis and why she ultimately chose not to pursue drug treatments. Instead, she focused on lifestyle changes including diet, exercise, and daily discipline. Along the way she faced several major challenges, all while continuing to pursue optimal health and eventually stepping onto the competitive bodybuilding stage in her 50s.

programme.title: Hope and Health With Mathew Embry
programme.desc: Angie Gensler is a mother and personal trainer who was diagnosed with multiple sclerosis in 2004. In this episode, Mathew Embry speaks with Angie about her diagnosis and why she ultimately chose not to pursue drug treatments. Instead, she focused on lifestyle changes including diet, exercise, and daily discipline. Along the way she faced several major challenges, all while continuing to pursue optimal health and eventually stepping onto the competitive bodybuilding stage in her 50s.

programme.desc: "Crush With Lee & Tiffany" stars outdoor enthusiasts Lee and Tiffany Lakosky, a husband/wife team whose partnership begins in the home and extends to the woods of eastern Iowa and beyond. They love to bowhunt, and when they are not in New Mexico tracking elk or in North Carolina chasing turkeys -- among other travels -- they fulfill their passion for the sport on their Iowa farm, where they practice a strict land-management program for world-class white-tailed deer. programme.title: Bone Collector
programme.desc: "Bone Collector" is one of the Outdoor Channel's most popular programs. Since premiering in 2009, the series has collected millions of fans by focusing on the culture of the hunter and not exclusively on the kill. According to host Michael Waddell, a hunter is more than a predator; a hunter is a provider, a caretaker, and a leader, and this show aims to promote that philosophy at every turn. Waddell is joined by co-hosts Nick Mundt and Travis "T-Bone" Turner for "warts and all" adventures that take them from Africa to Alaska, and the hunts are geared toward a variety of implements, including bow, muzzleloader, shotgun and rifle.

programme.desc: Misplaced bravado leads one badge to take down another; a whale of a tale about a sea creature far from home.

programme.title: MOJO TV
programme.desc: It's off to Rocky Creek Retrievers to experience early Blue Winged Teal and the regular waterfowl season.

programme.title: Fish TV
programme.desc: Brennan Harbour Resort.

programme.title: Big Water Angler
programme.desc: Taking viewers on an adventurous journey across the Great Lakes region and beyond, exploring the best fishing destinations, techniques and insider tips from seasoned captains and guides.

programme.title: Hunting Beast
programme.desc: Dan Infalt shares hunting tips and woodsman skills.

programme.title: Bone Collector
programme.desc: "Bone Collector" is one of the Outdoor Channel's most popular programs. Since premiering in 2009, the series has collected millions of fans by focusing on the culture of the hunter and not exclusively on the kill. According to host Michael Waddell, a hunter is more than a predator; a hunter is a provider, a caretaker, and a leader, and this show aims to promote that philosophy at every turn. Waddell is joined by co-hosts Nick Mundt and Travis "T-Bone" Turner for "warts and all" adventures that take them from Africa to Alaska, and the hunts are geared toward a variety of implements, including bow, muzzleloader, shotgun and rifle.

programme.title: Lisa Roper Outdoors
programme.desc: Welcome to Lisa Roper Outdoors, where every episode is a journey into the heart of outdoor adventure; from breathtaking landscapes to mouthwatering shore lunches, Lisa Roper brings you along as she connects with nature in a way that stirs the soul.

programme.title: Winchester & Drury's Natural Born
programme.desc: The Drury Outdoors Team hunts game and offers tips.

programme.title: Tracks Across Africa
programme.desc: Charlton McCallum Safaris PH Myles McCallum and hunter Terry Patrick are in Zimbabwe's Dande Safari Area on a wonderful mixed-bag safari for both dangerous and plains game species that call the Lower Zambezi Valley home.

programme.title: Elk Camp
programme.desc: An Arizona archery hunt with Elk Camp guide Tim Stanley and long time client Perry Berry.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Steve and his buddy Ronny Boehme head to southeast Wyoming, where they shoot clays with Gov. Matt Mead before heading out on a sage grouse hunt.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: During the cold closing days of elk season, Steve ascends thousands of feet into the Bitterroot Mountains of Western Montana seeking a legal bull.

programme.title: Jim Shockey's Shock Therapy
programme.desc: Jim sits down with John Dubin, a former FBI Counterterrorism Unit agent turned avid hunter and adventurer, to talk about his extraordinary journey from high-stakes operations to unforgettable hunts in one of the most spectacular places on earth.

programme.title: Jokers Wild off the Hook
programme.desc: Sparky Sparkes and his family travel to fish and have adventures in the U.S. and abroad.

programme.title: QVO - Quebec from a Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: A bird's eye view look at Quebec.

programme.title: Shooting USA
programme.desc: Reporter Jim Scoutten covers what fans and consumers wish to know about the shooting sports industry, including reports from national championships, field tests of product performance claims, history lessons from gun collectors, tips from professional marksmen, along with demonstrations from the world's top exhibition shooters. Also check out industry's newest inventory being subjected to review processes.

programme.title: Fishing the Flats
programme.desc: Henry Waszczuk provides tips on shallow water fishing in warm weather destinations.

programme.title: The Gamekeepers of Mossy Oak
programme.desc: We're joined in-studio by Dr. Blake Grisham of Texas Tech and Courtenay Conring, a dedicated young biologist. These two are leading a long-term research project on Sandhill Cranes.

programme.title: In Fisherman TV
programme.desc: The staff gets down to details that make a difference in the lure presentation process, offering overlooked tube rigging tricks for largemouth bass.

programme.title: Fins & Skins Classic Adventures
programme.desc: A unique mix between the sports celebrity guest, some great fishing and a Skins game in golf.

programme.title: Weekend Recharge
programme.desc: Though it has "weekend" in the title, this show discusses the week ahead in addition to offering suggestions for what to do on Saturday and Sunday. Weather Channel meteorologists go beyond the elements outside and into the realm of lifestyle tips. Each host has a recurring segment on "Weekend Recharge," which includes helping parents get a fresh start to each day and explaining how the weather is expected to impact the weekend's scheduled sports action.

programme.title: Pattrn
programme.desc: Elevating the conversation about climate, extreme weather, sustainability and green living.

programme.title: So You Think You'd Survive?
programme.desc: Incredible caught-on-tape moments challenge you with live power lines, dangerous sinkholes, enormous blizzards, massive landslides and vicious sunburns.

programme.title: So You Think You'd Survive?
programme.desc: Harrowing caught-on-camera video pits you against broken water mains, deadly lightning, dangerous hot air balloon crashes, and giant tsunamis!

programme.title: So You Think You'd Survive?
programme.desc: Thrilling video pits you against deadly desert heat, pounding white water rapids, dangerous thin ice, and lightning on a golf course.

programme.title: Could You Survive? With Creek Stewart
programme.desc: A young woman races for rescue as her boyfriend is trapped and slowly dying in a freezing Utah river; a hiker faces a fork in an Oregon trail, and the choice he makes leads to a five-day struggle for survival.

programme.title: Could You Survive? With Creek Stewart
programme.desc: A freak car accident leaves a man unable to move and fighting for his life, just a few hundred feet from a busy Houston highway; a skilled Florida hunter is out of his element, lost and alone in Alaska's punishing environment.

programme.title: Could You Survive? With Creek Stewart
programme.desc: A son's birthday present for his father turns a wilderness adventure into a life-or-death struggle against freezing weather and prowling grizzlies; a casual walk before work plunges a woman into a nightmare of raging rapids and swarms of insects.

programme.title: Heroes & Survivors
programme.desc: An adventure turns terrifying when a hiker gets stuck in quicksand; backcountry skiers fight for survival in a brutal blizzard; a surfer gets lost at sea; innocent animals fight for survival in hurricane aftermath.

programme.title: Heroes & Survivors
programme.desc: Caught in a savage storm, a day on a lake turns deadly; tourists must escape an epic flash flood; the hunt for kids trapped in a flooded cave; a rockslide threatens tourists.

programme.title: Tornado Alley
programme.desc: The story of an EF-5 tornado in Moore, Oklahoma; cell phone footage, local news reports, police dashboard cameras and personal narrative.

programme.title: Tornado Alley
programme.desc: The EF-5 tornado that struck Joplin, MO; horrific moments captured by those who survived.

programme.title: Tornado Alley
programme.desc: Stories of unbelievable survival in the face of a catastrophic tornado.

programme.title: Storm Stories: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: A mudslide in Washington state rips through a hillside community, killing 43 people; the strongest November tornado ever recorded in Illinois hits a small town, as a man races home to see if his children are alive amid the debris.

programme.title: Storm Stories: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: A local realtor heads out in his boat to rescue a woman and her family during Hurricane Harvey; in the midst of a violent winter storm, a harbormaster in a Massachusetts town falls into the water when he tries to tether a boat to the dock.

programme.title: Fat Guys in the Woods
programme.desc: Creek teaches three guys how to survive on a mountain slope primitive groups once called home thousands of years ago.

programme.title: Fat Guys in the Woods
programme.desc: Creek teaches Bill Anderson, Andrew Walker, and Dave Howard what it means to live off the land, just like the men before them.

programme.title: Fat Guys in the Woods
programme.desc: Creek teaches Opie, a guy looking to reconnect with his scouting past; Joe, a tech-obsessed New Yorker; Zach, a man who spent most of his life handicapped from a rare bone disease.

programme.desc: The science and stories of weather, plus in-depth forecasts with a focus on weekend weather.

programme.title: Action in the North Atlantic
programme.desc: Merchant Marine sailors Joe Rossi (Humphrey Bogart) and Steve Jarvis (Raymond Massey) are charged with getting a supply vessel to Russian allies as part of a sea convoy. When the group of ships comes under attack from a German U-boat, Rossi and Jarvis navigate through dangerous waters to evade Nazi naval forces. Though their mission across the Atlantic is extremely treacherous, they are motivated by the opportunity to strike back at the Germans, who sank one of their earlier ships.

programme.title: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: In this moving 1953 drama from director Melvin Frank, Col. Paul Tibbets, commander of the 509th Composite Group during World War II, struggles to come to grips with his peculiar place in human history: He's the first man to ever drop an atomic bomb. While his wife, Lucy, has stayed loyal to him, the aftermath of causing such destruction leaves Paul questioning his humanity and the mission that may have forever diminished it.

programme.title: 36 Hours
programme.desc: In occupied Germany in 1949, American military officer Jefferson Pike (James Garner) wakes up in a U.S. Army hospital with no memory of the previous five years. A kindly military psychiatrist (Rod Taylor) and his coolly detached nurse (Eva Marie Saint) draw out Pike's last clear memories, which are of General Eisenhower's final briefing for the Normandy invasion in June 1944. Soon, Pike realizes that his doctor and nurse are not who they claim to be, and that he is in great danger.

programme.title: The Green Berets
programme.desc: A cynical reporter (David Janssen) who is opposed to the Vietnam War is sent to cover the conflict and assigned to tag along with a group of Green Berets. Led by the tough-as-nails Col. Mike Kirby (John Wayne), the team is given a top-secret mission to sneak behind enemy lines and kidnap an important Viet Cong commander. Along the way, the reporter learns to respect why America is involved in the war and helps to save the life of a war orphan whose life has been destroyed by the conflict.

programme.title: Battle of the Bulge
programme.desc: American Lt. Col. Dan Kiley (Henry Fonda), a military intelligence whiz, discovers that the Nazis are planning to attack Allied forces near Belgium. Certain that the exhausted enemy can't muster much force, Gen. Joe Grey (Robert Ryan) isn't convinced by Kiley's findings, and his men pay the price when the German tanks begin their offensive. In the heat of this key World War II battle, Kiley must come up with a plan when it becomes clear that the Nazis are trying to steal fuel from the Allies.

programme.title: Betrayed
programme.desc: When the Dutch resistance brings in Carla Van Oven (Lana Turner) to spy on the Nazis, Col. Pieter Deventer (Clark Gable) initially suspects she could be a double agent. Van Oven eventually convinces Deventer of her character, and when she is sent into occupied territory, she joins up with a rebel leader known as "The Scarf" (Victor Mature). As the resistance endures severe losses and their missions fail, Deventer starts to wonder whose side Van Oven is fighting on.

programme.title: The Fast and the Furious
programme.desc: A framed man (John Ireland) takes a rich woman's (Dorothy Malone) Jaguar, with her in it, and enters a road race that ends in Mexico.

programme.title: Perry Stone

programme.desc: Join CSN Mint for a patriotic 2026 Silver Eagle overstruck with a 1776-2026 dual-date and American Flag Privy by Daniel Carr. Struck using a retired Denver Mint press and certified perfect MS70 First Strike by ANACS.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: A headstrong teenager named Jimmy Craig arrives in Dodge City and wants to become one of Earp's deputies, demonstrating his impeccable skill with a gun, but his dream contrasts sharply with his irrational obsession to duel Ed Beatty.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp takes Bat's brother Ed under his wing as a new deputy, but while Ed believes outlaws can be reasoned with, Bat knows better; two businessmen plan to kill Ed and frame Bat for the crime to secure ownership of a saloon.

programme.title: Scalplock
programme.desc: A frontier gambler (Dale Robertson) wins a railroad in a card game and has to keep it going as people try to take it away from him.

programme.title: Whispering Smith
programme.desc: Luke Whispering Smith (Alan Ladd) is a mild-mannered policeman tasked with stopping a gang of train robbers, led by Barney Rebstock (Donald Crisp). Luke is faced with a conflict of interest when his good friend, Murray Sinclair (Robert Preston), loses his railroad job and, in an act of defiance, joins Rebstock's gang of outlaws. To make things even more complicated, Luke's friendship with Murray's ex-wife, Marian (Brenda Marshall), is becoming romantic.

programme.title: Man of the West
programme.desc: Ex-outlaw Link Jones (Gary Cooper) is aboard a train when it is robbed by bandits. When he tries to intervene, he's knocked out and left in the middle of nowhere with saloon singer Billie (Julie London), whom he hoped to hire as a schoolteacher in his town, and Sam (Arthur O'Connell), a con man. Link brings the three to his nearby former home, now the hideout for the bandits, who are led by his uncle (Lee J. Cobb) -- and to save his new friends, he must rejoin the old gang for one last holdup.

programme.title: The Last Challenge
programme.desc: Hotshot gunfighter Lot McGuire intends to make a name for himself by out-duelling legendary bandit-turned-lawman Marshal Dan Blaine. Before any bullets fly, however, Lot and his intended enemy strike up an unlikely friendship, and Blaine tries to convince the youngster to call off the shootout. Unfortunately, Lot can't be talked out of his quest for glory, and so he guns down a few hostile locals, leaving Blaine no choice but to finally accept the offer for a showdown.

programme.title: Beach Party
programme.desc: Dolores (Annette Funicello) and Frankie (Frankie Avalon), attractive young surfers in Southern California, are just out for a good time. Their relaxed lifestyle fascinates Professor Sutwell (Bob Cummings), an anthropologist who decides to study the social lives of teens. A series of beach-themed films would follow the blueprint set by this one, which hinges on romantic rivalries, rugged motorcyclists, an attempted crime and plenty of handsome people.

programme.title: Bikini Beach
programme.desc: Dee Dee (Annette Funicello) and Frankie (Frankie Avalon) are primed to hit the beach for some fun in the sun. The only thing standing in the way of their good times is curmudgeonly developer Harvey Huntington Honeywagon (Keenan Wynn), who is intent on turning their teenage hotspot into a grazing ground for seniors. If that weren't bad enough, British rock 'n' roll singer Potato Bug (also Avalon) shows up to spark a summer romance with Frankie's main squeeze -- Dee Dee.

programme.title: Whispering Smith
programme.desc: A railroad detective (Alan Ladd) learns his ex-sweetheart's husband (Robert Preston) has joined a train robber's (Donald Crisp) gang.

programme.title: Man of the West
programme.desc: A reformed Texas outlaw (Gary Cooper), a con man and a singer (Julie London) meet the outlaw's old gang.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes unravels a murder involving a locked room with no key, a domestic disagreement and a set of animal tracks.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Rimfire
programme.desc: An undercover Army captain (James Millican) links missing gold and murder to a gambler's (Reed Hadley) ghost.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: When buffalo hunter Jake Caster arrives in Dodge City and an Army sergeant dies from an apparent Indian attack soon after, Earp investigates the tragedy that claimed the life of Jake's wife and child.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp tries to help gun repairman Guns McCallum deal with his temper by summoning his wife to Dodge City; when Guns admits he can't repair a gunslinger's favorite sidearm, he finds himself in a deadly showdown.

programme.title: Beach Party
programme.desc: An anthropologist studying the sex habits of young people finds himself in the middle of a pie-throwing brawl.

programme.title: Bikini Beach
programme.desc: A shaggy British pop star (Frankie Avalon) woos a look-alike beach bum's girlfriend (Annette Funicello).

programme.title: Desert Vigilante
programme.desc: The Durango Kid (Charles Starrett) bags Mexican-border silver smugglers for Uncle Sam.

programme.title: The Oklahoman
programme.desc: After Dr. John Brighton loses his wife in childbirth, he sets up his home in Indian territory. Some years later, two women compete for the doctor's affections: Ann, a ranch widow, and Maria, the young Native who takes care of John's daughter.

programme.title: The Last Challenge
programme.desc: An upstart outlaw (Chad Everett) baits a legendary gunslinger (Glenn Ford), now a marshal in love with a saloonkeeper (Angie Dickinson).

programme.title: Man in the Saddle
programme.desc: A wounded rancher (Randolph Scott) hides with a schoolmarm (Ellen Drew) after his ex-girlfriend's husband (Alexander Knox) runs him out of town.

programme.title: Dirty Dozen: The Next Mission
programme.desc: An Army major (Lee Marvin) can either do hard labor or lead GI convicts into France on a suicide mission.

programme.title: Aces High
programme.desc: Maj. Gresham (Malcolm McDowell) is a flying ace who oversees a group of English pilots during World War I. His latest recruit is Lt. Croft (Peter Firth), who happens to have been only a few years behind Gresham at Eton. Croft idolizes Gresham at first, but the image of his hero is tarnished when he learns of Gresham's private habits, especially his predilection for alcohol. It's only after Croft himself has endured the crucible of war that he comes to fully appreciate his commanding officer.

programme.title: The Oklahoman
programme.desc: While moving his family out West, Dr. John Brighton (Joel McCrea) loses his wife in childbirth. After her death, John sets up his home in Indian territory. By the time his daughter is 7 years old, two women are competing for the doctor's affections: Ann (Barbara Hale), a rancher's widow, and Maria (Gloria Talbott), a young Native American maiden who watches over the doctor's daughter. When the town bully, Mel (Douglas Dick), wants to take the land of Maria's father, the doctor gets involved.

programme.title: Desert Vigilante
programme.desc: The Durango Kid (Charles Starrett) bags Mexican-border silver smugglers for Uncle Sam.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes and Watson try to trace an undetectable murder weapon.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Doc Holliday, the gunslinging dentist, teams up with Earp to stop a gang of train robbers; Doc's wife, Kate, insists upon taking him to a hospital to receive treatment for consumption, but Earp can't take the gang alone.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp tries to resist pressure to throw Doc out of Dodge City, but when the dentist commits murder in the street, Earp throws him in jail and faces the ire of Doc's wife, Kate; Doc comes to Earp's rescue when a gang captures the marshal.

programme.title: Dirty Dozen: The Next Mission
programme.desc: An Army major (Lee Marvin) can either do hard labor or lead GI convicts into France on a suicide mission.

programme.title: Aces High
programme.desc: Maj. Gresham (Malcolm McDowell) is a flying ace who oversees a group of English pilots during World War I. His latest recruit is Lt. Croft (Peter Firth), who happens to have been only a few years behind Gresham at Eton. Croft idolizes Gresham at first, but the image of his hero is tarnished when he learns of Gresham's private habits, especially his predilection for alcohol. It's only after Croft himself has endured the crucible of war that he comes to fully appreciate his commanding officer.

programme.title: Yellowstone Kelly
programme.desc: Union soldier Maj. Towns (Rhodes Reason) asks fur trapper and Indian scout Luther "Yellowstone" Kelly (Clint Walker) to scout for the Army in Sioux territory. Kelly ventures into the area, only to be taken prisoner by the Sioux. Since Kelly saved the life of Chief Gall (John Russell) years before, the chief promises to free him if Kelly treats wounded Arapaho maiden Wahleeah (Andra Martin). Soon Kelly and the recovered Wahleeah are caught between Towns' soldiers and the vengeful Sioux.

programme.title: Man in the Saddle
programme.desc: A wounded rancher (Randolph Scott) hides with a schoolmarm (Ellen Drew) after his ex-girlfriend's husband (Alexander Knox) runs him out of town.

programme.title: Raw Edge
programme.desc: In 1842 lawless Oregon, a gunfighter seeks vengeance when his innocent brother is lynched and his beautiful Indian wife is claimed by the local tyrant.

programme.title: Three Hours to Kill
programme.desc: The inhabitants of a small western community turn on Jim Guthrie (Dana Andrews) when he becomes the prime suspect for a murder he didn't commit. Before getting a chance to prove his innocence, Jim is driven from his home and into the wilderness, where he is left for dead. Despite the danger, he comes back years later, discovering that he's still a wanted man. But a kindhearted local named Chris (Dianne Foster) believes his story, and together they begin a desperate search for the true culprit.

programme.title: The 7th Voyage of Sinbad
programme.desc: Sailing to Baghdad after a narrow escape from the monster island of Colossa, the wedding plans of legendary hero Sinbad and Princess Parisa are spoiled by the scheming sorcerer Sokurah. In return for his previous help with the cyclops on Colossa, Sokurah demands that Sinbad retrieve a lamp he lost on the island. When Sinbad refuses, the conjurer shrinks Parisa, forcing the sailor and his crew back to the high seas in order to save her.

programme.title: Yellowstone Kelly
programme.desc: Union soldier Maj. Towns (Rhodes Reason) asks fur trapper and Indian scout Luther "Yellowstone" Kelly (Clint Walker) to scout for the Army in Sioux territory. Kelly ventures into the area, only to be taken prisoner by the Sioux. Since Kelly saved the life of Chief Gall (John Russell) years before, the chief promises to free him if Kelly treats wounded Arapaho maiden Wahleeah (Andra Martin). Soon Kelly and the recovered Wahleeah are caught between Towns' soldiers and the vengeful Sioux.

programme.title: Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: After the death of its captain, seafarer Edmond Dantes successfully brings in the merchant vessel belonging to his employers only to find himself framed for the murder. Jailed on a remote island, he meets fellow prisoner Abbe Faria who provides Edmond with the education he never had as well as hope of escaping to find a buried fortune. When Edmond finally does break free, he locates the treasure and vows to avenge himself with it.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A battered hat and a Christmas goose are Holmes' only clues to clearing a falsely accused man.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: The Lonesome Trail
programme.desc: A group of conniving land-grabbers is deterred from further evil by a Western Robin Hood armed with a bow and arrow.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp must negotiate with Chief Dull Knife when a Cheyenne tribe launches an attack to retake their ancestral lands, hoping to avoid a confrontation with Maj. Benteen's soldiers.

programme.title: Paid Programming: Carshield -- Cars Have Changed
The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: When Doc purchases a gold brick from a wanted thief and then sells it to prove a point, Earp follows the brick's trail as it passes through the hands of various Dodge City residents.

programme.title: The 7th Voyage of Sinbad
programme.desc: Sailing to Baghdad after a narrow escape from the monster island of Colossa, the wedding plans of legendary hero Sinbad and Princess Parisa are spoiled by the scheming sorcerer Sokurah. In return for his previous help with the cyclops on Colossa, Sokurah demands that Sinbad retrieve a lamp he lost on the island. When Sinbad refuses, the conjurer shrinks Parisa, forcing the sailor and his crew back to the high seas in order to save her.

programme.title: Forbidden Trail
programme.desc: A practical-joking cowboy (Buck Jones) gets serious with outlaws.

programme.title: Roughshod
programme.desc: Rancher Clay (Robert Sterling) and his brother, Steve (Claude Jarman Jr.), head out across the Sonora mountain pass, followed by Lednov (John Ireland), an ex-con seeking revenge on Clay for putting him behind bars. Clay and Steve unexpectedly cross paths with a group of dance hall girls -- including Mary (Gloria Grahame), Marcia (Martha Hyer) and Helen (Myrna Dell) -- whose stagecoach has broken down, and help them get to the nearest ranch, where Lednov unfortunately catches up to Clay.

programme.title: Three Hours to Kill
programme.desc: The inhabitants of a small western community turn on Jim Guthrie (Dana Andrews) when he becomes the prime suspect for a murder he didn't commit. Before getting a chance to prove his innocence, Jim is driven from his home and into the wilderness, where he is left for dead. Despite the danger, he comes back years later, discovering that he's still a wanted man. But a kindhearted local named Chris (Dianne Foster) believes his story, and together they begin a desperate search for the true culprit.

programme.title: Raw Edge
programme.desc: In 1842 lawless Oregon, a gunfighter seeks vengeance when his innocent brother is lynched and his beautiful Indian wife is claimed by the local tyrant.

programme.title: Trail Street
programme.desc: Legendary lawman Bat Masterson (Randolph Scott) is called to rural Kansas to defend farmers from ruthless cattlemen. Joining Masterson in his efforts to clean up a lawless town are a couple of locals: Masterson's old chum Billy Burn and landowner Allen Harper (Robert Ryan). But ranch owner Logan Maury proves to be a more than formidable opponent. He'll stop at nothing, including murder, to turn the farmers' fields into grazing grounds for his cattle.

programme.title: Father of the Bride
programme.desc: When beautiful Kay Banks (Elizabeth Taylor) announces her engagement to Buckley Dunstan (Don Taylor), her doting middle-class father, Stan (Spencer Tracy), must contend with a variety of problems, ranging from money issues to wedding planning difficulties. As things get hectic, Stan's wife, Ellie (Joan Bennett), tries to be the calm in the center of the storm. At the heart of the comedy, though, is Stan's emotional tie to his little girl, and his realization that she has indeed grown up.

programme.title: Jason and the Argonauts
programme.desc: After saving the life of his royal father's usurper, Pelias (Douglas Wilmer), whom he fails to recognize, Jason (Todd Armstrong) is encouraged by the conniving murderer to begin a quest in search of the Golden Fleece. In doing so, Pelias hopes for Jason's death to thwart the prophecy that he will reclaim the throne. Along the journey, Jason is protected by the goddess Hera (Honor Blackman), and his crew includes Hercules (Nigel Green) and Acastus (Gary Raymond), a saboteur planted by Pelias.

programme.title: Roughshod
programme.desc: Rancher Clay (Robert Sterling) and his brother, Steve (Claude Jarman Jr.), head out across the Sonora mountain pass, followed by Lednov (John Ireland), an ex-con seeking revenge on Clay for putting him behind bars. Clay and Steve unexpectedly cross paths with a group of dance hall girls -- including Mary (Gloria Grahame), Marcia (Martha Hyer) and Helen (Myrna Dell) -- whose stagecoach has broken down, and help them get to the nearest ranch, where Lednov unfortunately catches up to Clay.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes and the Secret Weapon
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes (Basil Rathbone) saves noted Swiss physicist Franz Tobel (William Post Jr.) from the Gestapo in Zurich and smuggles him to London. There, Tobel is reunited with his wife, Charlotte (Kaaren Verne), to whom he gives a coded message in the event he disappears. Tobel presents the weapon he developed to government officials, and when he is subsequently abducted by arch-villain Professor Moriarty (Lionel Atwill), Holmes and Dr. Watson (Nigel Bruce) risk their lives to find him.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: S05 E01 Waste Lucas, Mark and Micah are taken prisoner by a group of bandits.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: S05 E02 Waste Lucas, Mark and Micah escape from bandits and take refuge in a deserted building.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: S05 E03 Lou Mallory Lou Mallory, a stunning redhead, arrives in North Fork and buys the hotel.

programme.title: The Cowboy Way
programme.desc: S07 E07 Northern Exposure The cowboys partner with a successful rancher in northern Alabama; Misty mentors a junior barrel racer; Bubba trains his new ranch horses in hopes of selling them at a profit; Booger decides to cook up something special for his grandma.

programme.title: The Cowboy Way
programme.desc: S07 E08 Gator Raid With herds in two states, the cowboys must split up to handle all the work; Booger and Bubba track down a predator preying on calves in Florida; Cody processes a cattle shipment in Alabama; the gang helps Matthew with a school science project.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: S03 E04 The Pitchman A con man and his son want Lucas' mineral rights.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: S03 E05 Strange Town Lucas trails an escaped prisoner to a mining town in the mountains.

programme.title: F Troop
programme.desc: S01 E19 El Diablo Agarn goes on a manhunt for his look-alike cousin, the notorious bandit El Diablo.

programme.title: F Troop
programme.desc: S01 E20 Go for Broke Capt. Parmenter plays poker to win back F Troop's lost pension fund.

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.desc: S01 E12 Annie Oakley: Rise of a Shooting Star When a young Annie Oakley accepts a challenge to face one of the best sharpshooters in the country, she wins the match and his heart. Annie Oakley marries trick-shot artist Frank Butler, and they struggle to make it in show business.

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.desc: S01 E15 Charles M. Russell: The Cowboy Artist Charlie Russell is an aimless cowhand who enjoys painting scenes of his life on the range until his new bride, Nancy, gives him the push he needs to stop cowboying in order to focus on the artwork that will soon make him famous.

programme.title: Into the Wild Frontier
programme.desc: S01 E04 Lewis & Clark: Captains of Discovery As Meriwether Lewis and William Clark trek into the uncharted West, the rivers become too shallow for boats; they must find friendly Native Americans willing to trade for horses or risk spending winter on the wrong side of the Rocky Mountains.

programme.title: The Young Riders
programme.desc: S03 E18 The Sacrifice Hickok and Teaspoon realize their friendship with a dying black man will transcend the impending Civil War.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: S02 E20 Yellow River A beautiful con woman hires Bart as a trail boss.

programme.title: Cheyenne
programme.desc: S03 E12 The Empty Gun Cheyenne teams up with an ex-gunslinger to deliver thousands of dollars to the widow of the man he killed years earlier.

programme.title: Bonanza
programme.desc: S01 E29 Bitter Water When a neighbor agrees to sell his property, the Cartwrights fear the loss of water rights for their cattle.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: S05 E04 Quiet Night, Deadly Night Lucas and several others are trapped in the hotel with a dangerous outlaw.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: S05 E05 Death Never Rides Alone A former gunfighter arrives in North Fork with the intention of settling down.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: S05 E06 I Take This Woman A charming Irishman claims Lou for his bride.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: S05 E07 The Assailants Lucas gets wind of an assassination plot.

programme.title: The Cowboy Way
programme.desc: S07 E07 Northern Exposure The cowboys partner with a successful rancher in northern Alabama; Misty mentors a junior barrel racer; Bubba trains his new ranch horses in hopes of selling them at a profit; Booger decides to cook up something special for his grandma.

Dennis Gage, the handlebar mustachioed host, takes you along for the ride and keeps it entertaining with his unique perspective and enthusiasm for all things automotive. This show has something for everyone. From daily-drivers to Duesenbergs - from local cruise-ins to the most prestigious car shows in the world, this program covers it all!

programme.title: Racers Unchained
programme.desc: Tony Kanaan and Paul Tracy discuss their careers in open-wheel racing; Kanaan, an Indianapolis 500 winner and IndyCar champion, serves as the team principal for Arrow McLaren while the two drivers review their experiences in the sport.

programme.title: Road to Glory
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes and get to know the drivers behind the helmet; from seasoned veterans to young upstarts, each racer has a different path but one goal -- to win the title.

programme.title: Manufacturing Death: Birth of the Atom Bomb
programme.desc: A look at how the birth of the atomic bomb changed the world by bringing an end to the largest conflict in history and ushering in an atomic age and a defining era of big science.

programme.title: Victory at Sea
programme.desc: A look back at WWII's combat at sea, featuring battle footage, scenes of everyday life on aircraft carriers, and more.

programme.title: The World Seen From the Vatican
programme.desc: All the latest news about Pope, the Vatican, and other related social and political issues.

programme.title: Finding the Mountain of Moses: The Real Mt. Sinai
programme.desc: New archaeological evidence and research based on Christian, Jewish and Islamic sources indicates the real location of Mount Sinai is in Saudi Arabia.
